content: STCA (Short Term Conflict Alert, SKYbrary/EUROCONTROL) predicts, from current track+velocity data, whether minimum separation will be violated within a short look-ahead window (commonly ~2 minutes), rather than only checking current-instant separation. Naive linear (straight-line) trajectory prediction is the most common approach but produces excess nuisance/false alerts during turns, climbs, and descents in terminal airspace where maneuvering is frequent — tuning/filtering (or multi-hypothesis prediction accounting for turn intent) is needed to keep the false-alert rate operationally acceptable without suppressing genuine conflicts.
